raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "The statement functions as a commitment to a specific spiritual practice aimed at deepening relationship with a higher power, focusing on alignment with that power's will rather than on personal requests.",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "A higher power (God) exists and is personally accessible.",
    "This higher power has a will for the individual.",
    "Prayer and meditation are effective means of improving conscious contact with that higher power.",
    "It is appropriate and beneficial to seek only knowledge of this will and the power to carry it out, rather than seeking specific outcomes.",
    "The individual has the capacity to discern and carry out such a will with the power granted."
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "The statement is internally coherent: it defines a method (prayer and meditation), a goal (improving conscious contact), a limitation (praying only for knowledge and power), and a desired outcome (alignment with divine will). The logic is self-consistent within a theistic framework.",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": [
    "The individual accepts the existence of a personal higher power.",
    "The individual is willing to engage in regular prayer and meditation.",
    "The individual is open to receiving and acting on guidance perceived as coming from that higher power.",
    "The individual has a framework for interpreting experiences (e.g., intuition, conscience) as communication of divine will."
  ],
  "conditions_for_misleading": [
    "If the higher power does not exist or is not responsive, the practice may lead to false certainty or misplaced trust.",
    "If the individual's understanding of 'God' is vague or contradictory, the practice may cause confusion or reinforce harmful beliefs.",
    "If the individual mistakes personal desire or psychological projection for divine will, the statement could justify harmful actions.",
    "If used to defer personal responsibility or avoid critical thinking, the statement could be misleading."
  ]
}

parsed_json.communicative_function: The statement functions as a commitment to a specific spiritual practice aimed at deepening relationship with a higher power, focusing on alignment with that power's will rather than on personal requests.
implicit_claims: A higher power (God) exists and is personally accessible.
This higher power has a will for the individual.
Prayer and meditation are effective means of improving conscious contact with that higher power.
It is appropriate and beneficial to seek only knowledge of this will and the power to carry it out, rather than seeking specific outcomes.
The individual has the capacity to discern and carry out such a will with the power granted.
parsed_json.coherence_assessment: The statement is internally coherent: it defines a method (prayer and meditation), a goal (improving conscious contact), a limitation (praying only for knowledge and power), and a desired outcome (alignment with divine will). The logic is self-consistent within a theistic framework.
conditions_for_usefulness: The individual accepts the existence of a personal higher power.
The individual is willing to engage in regular prayer and meditation.
The individual is open to receiving and acting on guidance perceived as coming from that higher power.
The individual has a framework for interpreting experiences (e.g., intuition, conscience) as communication of divine will.
conditions_for_misleading: If the higher power does not exist or is not responsive, the practice may lead to false certainty or misplaced trust.
If the individual's understanding of 'God' is vague or contradictory, the practice may cause confusion or reinforce harmful beliefs.
If the individual mistakes personal desire or psychological projection for divine will, the statement could justify harmful actions.
If used to defer personal responsibility or avoid critical thinking, the statement could be misleading.
